sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Good, the board changed after clicking on the yellow square. Let me see what changed by comparing the before and after frames.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Compare before and after to see what changed
before = last_transition.before_frame
after = last_transition.after_frame

# Get segmentation for both
before_seg = before.segmentation
after_seg = after.segmentation

# Compare node lists
before_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in before_seg['nodes']}
after_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in after_seg['nodes']}

print(f"Before: {len(before_nodes)} nodes, After: {len(after_nodes)} nodes")

# Check for color/shape changes in the yellow area
print("\nChecking yellow node (node 10):")
if 10 in before_nodes and 10 in after_nodes:
    bn = before_nodes[10]
    an = after_nodes[10]
    print(f"  Before: color={bn['color']}, pixels={bn['pixels']}, boundary={bn['boundary']}")
    print(f"  After:  color={an['color']}, pixels={an['pixels']}, boundary={an['boundary']}")

# Check all nodes for changes
print("\nChanged nodes:")
for nid in sorted(set(list(before_nodes.keys()) + list(after_nodes.keys()))):
    if nid in before_nodes and nid in after_nodes:
        bn = before_nodes[nid]
        an = after_nodes[nid]
        if bn['color'] != an['color'] or bn['pixels'] != an['pixels'] or bn['boundary'] != an['boundary']:
            print(f"  Node {nid}: color {bn['color']}->{an['color']}, pixels {bn['pixels']}->{an['pixels']}")
    elif nid in before_nodes:
        print(f"  Node {nid}: REMOVED (was {before_nodes[nid]['color']})")
    else:
        print(f"  Node {nid}: ADDED ({after_nodes[nid]['color']})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
